title: Reported Speech (Estilo Indirecto)
description: Turn direct quotes into reported speech: decir que + a clause, the tense backshift when the reporting verb is in the past (estoy → estaba, iré → iría), the pronoun, possessive, time and place shifts (hoy → aquel día), reporting questions with preguntar si and question words, and reporting commands with the subjunctive.

cards.back: He said (that) he was tired — reporting a statement with decir que
cards.notes: To report a statement, use a reporting verb + **que** + a clause: *Dijo que...*, *Comentó que...*, *Explicó que...*. Unlike English, the **que** is never dropped: *Dice que viene* (He says (that) he is coming).

cards.back: Present reporting verb → no tense shift
cards.notes: When the reporting verb is in the **present** (or future), there is **no backshift**: *Dice que está cansado* (He says he is tired). The shift only happens when the reporting verb is in the **past**.

cards.back: present → imperfect (backshift after a past reporting verb)
cards.notes: After a **past** reporting verb, the **present** becomes the **imperfect**: *estoy → estaba*, *vivo → vivía*. This mirrors English ("I am tired" → he said he **was** tired), so it feels natural.

cards.front: "Fui / He ido" → Dijo que había ido.
cards.notes: Both the **pretérito indefinido** (*fui*) and the **pretérito perfecto** (*he ido*) shift to the **pluscuamperfecto**: *había ido*. English does the same: "I went / I have gone" → he said he **had gone**.

The **future** becomes the **conditional**: *iré → iría*, *vendré → vendría*. Again, like English: "I will go" → he said he **would** go. The conditional endings (-ía, -ías, -ía, -íamos, -íais, -ían) are the same for all verbs.

cards.front: "Yo tengo mi libro" → Dijo que él tenía su libro.
cards.back: shift pronouns and possessives: yo → él, mi → su
cards.notes: When you report someone else's words, the **pronouns and possessives** move to the third person: *yo → él/ella*, *me → le*, *mi → su*, *nosotros → ellos*. Verbs follow: *tengo → tenía*.

cards.front: hoy → aquel día; aquí → allí; mañana → al día siguiente
cards.back: shift time and place words when reporting later/elsewhere
cards.notes: Words tied to the original moment shift: *hoy → aquel día*, *ayer → el día anterior*, *mañana → al día siguiente*, *ahora → entonces*, *aquí → allí*, *este → aquel*. Use them only when the time or place has actually changed.

cards.front: "¿Tienes tiempo?" → Me preguntó si tenía tiempo.
cards.back: report a yes/no question with preguntar si
cards.notes: Yes/no questions become **preguntar + si** (whether): *¿Tienes tiempo?* → *Me preguntó **si** tenía tiempo*. Note: no question marks, no inversion, and the verb still backshifts (*tienes → tenía*).

cards.back: report a wh-question: keep the question word (with accent), no inversion
cards.notes: Questions with a question word **keep** it (*qué, dónde, cuándo, quién, cómo, por qué*), still written **with its accent**: *Me preguntó **dónde** vivía*. Spanish has no auxiliary "do" and no subject-verb inversion: just *dónde vivía*, not *dónde vives tú*.

cards.back: report a command with que + imperfect subjunctive
cards.notes: An **imperative** becomes **que + imperfect subjunctive**: *¡Ven!* → *Me dijo que **viniera***. There is no English parallel ("He told me to come" uses an infinitive), so this is a classic trap. The imperfect subjunctive ends in -ara/-iera.

cards.back: pedir/decir que + subjunctive to report a request or order
cards.notes: Reporting requests and orders uses **pedir / decir + que + subjunctive**: *Me pidió que llegara temprano* (He asked me to arrive early). Note *decir que* takes the **indicative** for statements but the **subjunctive** for commands.

cards.front: Me preguntó qué quería, NOT qué hacía querer.
cards.back: no auxiliary 'do' and no inversion in reported questions
cards.notes: English adds *do/does/did* and inverts ("what **did** you want"); Spanish does neither. Keep simple statement word order after the question word: *Me preguntó **qué quería***, *Me preguntó **cuándo llegaba el tren***.

steps.title: From quote to report
steps.body:
# Reported Speech — *Estilo Indirecto*

**Direct speech** quotes the exact words: *Pablo dijo: "Estoy cansado".*
**Reported (indirect) speech** folds them into a sentence with a reporting verb + **que**: *Pablo dijo **que estaba** cansado.*

Two things to remember from the start:

1. The **que** is obligatory. English can drop "that" (*He said he was tired*), Spanish cannot (*Dijo **que** estaba cansado*).
2. The backshift only happens when the reporting verb is in the **past** (*dijo, comentó, preguntó*). With a present reporting verb there is **no shift**:

- *Dice que **está** cansado.* (He says he is tired — no shift)
- *Dijo que **estaba** cansado.* (He said he was tired — shift)

> Good news for English speakers: the backshift works almost exactly like English ("is" → "was", "will" → "would"), so most of it will feel familiar.

steps.title: The tense backshift
steps.body:
## The backshift, after a past reporting verb

When the reporting verb is in the past (*dijo, explicó, preguntó*), the verb inside shifts back one step:

| Direct speech | Reported speech | Example |
|---|---|---|
| presente (*estoy*) | imperfecto (*estaba*) | Dijo que **estaba** cansado. |
| pret. perfecto (*he ido*) | pluscuamperfecto (*había ido*) | Dijo que **había ido**. |
| pret. indefinido (*fui*) | pluscuamperfecto (*había ido*) | Dijo que **había ido**. |
| futuro (*iré*) | condicional (*iría*) | Dijo que **iría**. |
| imperativo (*¡ven!*) | que + imperf. subj. (*viniera*) | Me dijo que **viniera**. |

Notice that the **imperfect** and the **conditional** themselves don't shift further — they're already the "back" forms.

> Mnemonic, just like English: *is → was*, *went/has gone → had gone*, *will → would*.

steps.title: Pronouns, time/place, questions and commands
steps.body:
## Pronouns, possessives, time and place

Reporting from a new viewpoint also shifts **who** and **when/where**:

- Person: *yo → él/ella*, *mi → su*, *nosotros → ellos*, *me → le*.
- Time: *hoy → aquel día*, *ayer → el día anterior*, *mañana → al día siguiente*, *ahora → entonces*.
- Place: *aquí → allí*, *este → aquel*.

*"Hoy estoy aquí con mi familia"* → *Dijo que **aquel día estaba allí con su** familia.*

## Reporting questions

- **Yes/no** questions → **preguntar si**: *¿Tienes tiempo?* → *Me preguntó **si** tenía tiempo.*
- **Wh-** questions → keep the question word (with its accent): *¿Dónde vives?* → *Me preguntó **dónde** vivía.*

Crucially, Spanish has **no auxiliary "do"** and **no inversion**: it's *Me preguntó qué quería*, never *qué hacía querer* or *qué quería él*.

## Reporting commands and requests

An imperative has no indicative to shift to — it becomes **que + imperfect subjunctive**:

- *¡Ven!* → *Me dijo que **viniera**.*
- *Por favor, llega temprano* → *Me pidió que **llegara** temprano.*

There's no English parallel (English uses *told me **to come***), so this is the lesson's main trap: a reported command needs the **subjunctive**, not the indicative.

exercise.prompt: Match each direct-speech verb with the tense it shifts to in reported speech (after a past reporting verb).
Report it: *He said (that) he was tired.* (direct: "Estoy cansado")
exercise.hint: decir que + clause; present estoy → imperfect estaba.

exercise.prompt: Direct: "He ido al banco". Fill in the pluperfect to report it.
exercise.sentence: Me dijo que ___ ido al banco.
exercise.hint: he ido / fui → había ido (pluscuamperfecto); haber for él = había.

steps.title: Build: she said she would come

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words to report "Vendré": *She said (that) she would come.*
exercise.hint: Future vendré → conditional vendría; decir que + clause.

exercise.prompt: Direct: "¡Ven!". Which correctly reports *He told me to come*? (command → subjunctive)
exercise.hint: A reported command needs que + imperfect subjunctive: viniera (not the indicative venía).

exercise.prompt: Report it: *She asked me if I had time.* (direct: "¿Tienes tiempo?")
exercise.hint: Yes/no question → preguntar si; tienes → tenía.

exercise.prompt: Direct: "¿Dónde vives?". Fill in the question word (with its accent) to report it.
exercise.hint: Keep the question word with its accent: dónde. No inversion, no auxiliary do.

exercise.prompt: Match each direct-speech word with its reported-speech equivalent.
Report it: *He asked me where I lived.* (direct: "¿Dónde vives?") — no inversion, no "do".
exercise.hint: Keep dónde (with accent); vives → vivía; no inversion, no auxiliary.
distractors: Me preguntó dónde vivía yo vivía.

exercise.prompt: Direct: "Llega temprano". Fill in the imperfect subjunctive of *llegar* to report the request.
exercise.hint: pedir que + imperfect subjunctive: llegar → llegara (or llegase).

steps.title: Report a command — watch the subjunctive

exercise.prompt: Report it: *She told me to call her.* (direct: "Llámame") — command → que + subjunctive.
exercise.hint: Reported command → que + imperfect subjunctive: llamar → llamara/llamase; la = her.

steps.title: Build: he asked me if I was coming

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words to report "¿Vienes?": *He asked me if I was coming.*
exercise.hint: Yes/no question → preguntar si; vienes → venía.
